fields.description: Enable or disable the Frappe Assistant Core MCP server
fields.label: Enable Assistant Core

fields.description: Main MCP StreamableHTTP endpoint URL. Use this URL when connecting Claude Desktop or MCP Inspector.
fields.label: MCP Endpoint URL

fields.description: OAuth/OIDC discovery endpoint for auto-configuration. MCP clients use this to discover authentication endpoints.
fields.label: OAuth Discovery URL

fields.description: Manage MCP tool plugins. Plugins extend the MCP server with additional tools and capabilities.
fields.label: Plugin Configuration

fields.description: Internal list of enabled plugin IDs
fields.label: Enabled Plugins

fields.description: Current status of installed plugins and available tools
fields.label: Plugin Status

fields.description: Configure resource limits for the run_python_code tool to prevent runaway code from crashing the system. These limits protect against infinite loops, memory exhaustion, and excessive recursion.
fields.label: Code Execution Limits

fields.description: Maximum wall-clock time allowed for code execution. Code that exceeds this limit will be terminated. Range: 1-300 seconds.
fields.label: Execution Timeout (seconds)

fields.description: Maximum memory allowed for code execution. Prevents memory exhaustion attacks. Range: 64-2048 MB.
fields.label: Max Memory (MB)

fields.description: Maximum CPU time allowed. Unlike wall-clock timeout, this limits actual processing time. Range: 1-300 seconds.
fields.label: Max CPU Time (seconds)

fields.description: Maximum recursion depth allowed. Prevents stack overflow from deeply recursive code. Range: 50-1000. Frappe internals (frappe.get_doc, frappe.get_all) recurse well past 100, so keep this at the default unless you have a reason to lower it.
fields.label: Max Recursion Depth
Limits Info
fields.options: Note: These limits use OS-level resource controls (signals, rlimit) and only work on Unix-like systems (Linux, macOS). On Windows, limits are not enforced. Changes take effect immediately for new code executions.

fields.description: Configure how long audit logs are retained. A daily scheduled job automatically cleans up logs older than the configured retention period.
fields.label: Audit Log Retention

fields.description: Number of days to retain audit logs. Logs older than this are automatically cleaned up daily. Set to 0 to disable automatic cleanup.
fields.label: Retention Period (Days)

fields.description: PaddleOCR is the default OCR engine for extracting text from scanned documents and images. Ollama vision LLM is available as an optional alternative for complex documents.
fields.label: OCR Configuration

fields.description: OCR engine to use for text extraction from images and scanned PDFs.
fields.label: OCR Backend

fields.description: Default language for PaddleOCR. Common codes: en, fr, german, es, ch. See PaddleOCR docs for full list.
fields.label: OCR Language

fields.description: Maximum time in seconds for PaddleOCR to process a document. Includes model loading time (~10s). Increase for large or complex documents.
fields.label: PaddleOCR Timeout (seconds)

fields.description: Maximum memory in MB for the PaddleOCR subprocess. The process will be killed if it exceeds this limit, protecting the Frappe worker from out-of-memory crashes.
fields.label: PaddleOCR Max Memory (MB)

fields.description: Configure the Ollama server and vision model used for OCR. Requires Ollama running locally with a vision-capable model.
fields.label: Ollama Vision Settings

fields.description: Ollama API server URL.
fields.label: Ollama API URL

fields.description: Vision model to use for OCR. Must be a model that supports image input (e.g., deepseek-ocr, llava, moondream).
fields.label: Vision Model

fields.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for Ollama to process a single page. Increase for slow hardware or large documents.
fields.label: Request Timeout (seconds)

fields.description: Configure MCP (Model Context Protocol) server identity and capabilities. These values are reported to MCP clients during initialization and discovery.
fields.label: MCP Server Settings

fields.description: Unique identifier for this MCP server instance. Used in MCP initialization handshake.
fields.label: MCP Server Name

fields.description: MCP specification version this server implements. Currently supports 2025-06-18.
fields.label: MCP Protocol Version

fields.description: Transport protocol used for MCP communication. StreamableHTTP enables OAuth-based authentication.
fields.label: Transport Type

fields.description: Supplementary: tools keep full descriptions, skills provide additional guidance. Replace: tool descriptions are minimized when a skill exists, saving tokens.
fields.label: Skill Mode

fields.description: Configure OAuth 2.0 settings for MCP clients (e.g., Claude Desktop, MCP Inspector)
fields.label: OAuth Configuration

fields.description: Enable automatic OAuth client registration for MCP tools. When enabled, tools like MCP Inspector can connect without manual OAuth client setup.
fields.label: Enable Dynamic Client Registration

fields.description:
⚠️ EXPERIMENTAL - Use site_config.json instead (recommended)

For MCP Inspector testing only. Claude and Chatgpt do NOT need this.

Use this field (one origin per line). Leave blank for production.
fields.label: Allowed Public Client Origins (Experimental)

fields.description: Controls which OAuth discovery endpoints are exposed. Most users should leave these enabled.
fields.label: Advanced Discovery Settings

fields.description: Exposes OAuth server capabilities at /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server. Required for auto-discovery.
fields.label: Show Authorization Server Metadata

fields.description: Exposes resource server info at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
fields.label: Show Protected Resource Metadata

fields.description: Display name for your resource server
fields.label: Resource Name

fields.description: Documentation URL for developers
fields.label: Documentation URL

fields.description: Privacy policy URL
fields.label: Policy URI

fields.description: Terms of service URL
fields.label: Terms of Service URI

fields.description: Supported OAuth scopes (one per line). Leave blank to use default scopes (all, openid).
fields.label: Supported Scopes
